$comment: SP-005 SearchHit canonical JSON Schema. Source of truth is SearchHitZodSchema in packages/contracts/src/search-schemas.ts. This file is committed for auditor review; any divergence between this file and the Zod schema is a bug. The full corpus.find response shape is { hits: SearchHit[], query: string, result_count: int, tier_used: 'hybrid', signals_used: string[], degraded_signals?: string[], filters_applied?: SearchFilters, error?: SearchErrorEnvelope } — this schema covers a single entry in the hits array.
title: SearchHit
description: A single entry in the corpus.find response's `hits` array. Carries enough metadata for the caller to (a) display the hit to a user, (b) dereference the underlying document via corpus://docs/{id} (SP-002 read path), (c) understand the ranking via the score field.

uri.description: Stable URI for the document. Always of the form `corpus://docs/doc-XXXXXXXX` where the 8-hex-char id matches `documents.id`. Resolvable via the SP-002 `corpus://docs/{id}` MCP resource (RFC-6570 template). This is the canonical dereferencing target — clients fetch the body + frontmatter via the resource template, NOT by re-parsing the URI.

score.description: Final fused + confidence-adjusted score. Positive number. NOT normalized to [0, 1] — RRF scores are sums of `1 / (k + rank)` (each term in [0, 1/61]), then multiplied by confidence weight (∈ [0.85, 1.25]) and recency adjustment (∈ [0.90, 1.05]). Higher = more relevant. The score is meaningful only for relative ordering within a single response; absolute values are not comparable across queries.

title.description: Document title. Sourced from documents.title (SP-003-owned, typically the filename basename). NOT generated by an LLM (Principle II).

facet_domain.description: Primary subject area. From documents.facet_domain (SP-004-populated; one of the established-vocabulary terms in taxonomy_terms WHERE state='established').

facet_type.description: Structural kind of document per SCHEMA.md v1.0 7-value enum. Constitutional taxonomy axis (FR-CLASSIFY-014). From documents.facet_type (SP-004-populated).

tags.description: 3-10 grep-able keywords. From documents.tags_json (SP-004-populated). Each entry is in EstablishedVocabulary.tags.

snippet.description: BM25-derived snippet from the matched body_excerpt; first 200 chars with `<mark>...</mark>` highlight markers around matched terms (or empty string if no BM25 match contributed). Bounded to 400 chars for response-size management. The snippet is generated at retrieval time; never persisted to documents.tags_json or documents_fts beyond the standard body_excerpt column.
